applied_optimizations.name: Optimized Triton kernel with vectorized processing
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented optimized Triton kernel that processes BLOCK_SIZE=256 output elements per program. Key optimizations: (1) Vectorized index decoding using tl.arange for parallel position computation, (2) Efficient stride-based position calculation, (3) Proper handling of padding with clamped indices and validity masks, (4) Division by kernel_volume (27) to match PyTorch AvgPool3d behavior with padding

attempted_but_failed.name: Tiled kernel with multiple output dimensions per program
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton doesn't support 'continue' statement in kernels, caused compilation error

attempted_but_failed.name: BLOCK_SIZE=512
attempted_but_failed.reason: GPU memory access fault - likely register pressure or shared memory issues

top_bottlenecks: Initial kernel had redundant index computation per iteration
PyTorch AvgPool3d is highly optimized native kernel

notes: PyTorch AvgPool3d with padding divides by kernel_volume (27), not by valid element count
Initial kernel was 2.8% slower than PyTorch (5.89ms vs 5.73ms)
Key insight: vectorize index computation across BLOCK_SIZE threads rather than computing per-element
